candidates.notes: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10. Strong conservative record: cosponsored Life at Conception Act H.R. 431; Iowa Senate sponsor of the 2018 fetal-heartbeat ban SF 359; Yea H.R. 26 Born-Alive (2023 roll 29); NAY on the Respect for Marriage Act (2022 roll 513) citing religious-liberty threats to churches/nonprofits - the conservative side where IA colleagues Hinson and Miller-Meeks voted Yea; Yea H.R. 28 (2025 roll 12), H.R. 5 Parents Bill of Rights (2023 roll 161), SAVE Act (2025 roll 102), H.R. 2 border (2023 roll 209), CBDC Anti-Surveillance (2024 roll 230), H.J.Res. 44 pistol-brace CRA (2023 roll 252). candidates.notes: Senator since 1981. Longest-serving Republican senator in history. Heritage Action 94% current (much higher than 66% lifetime — voting more conservatively in recent years). NRA A+ rated. Former Senate Judiciary Committee Chair. Pro-life. Supports border security. Known for holding town halls in all 99 Iowa counties annually. Active farmer. Famous Twitter presence. Some past compromises on immigration reform lowered lifetime Heritage score. Speaker of the Iowa House — verified via https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Speaker_of_the_Iowa_House_of_Representatives.

profile.education: University of Northern Iowa (BA Political Science, MA Political Science)
profile.background: Longest-serving Republican senator. Family farmer. Former Iowa state legislator and U.S. House member. In Senate since 1981. President pro tempore emeritus. Known for whistleblower advocacy.

candidates.name: Rob Sand Senate
candidates.office: U.S. Senate Iowa (2026 D Candidate · IA State Auditor · also Gov candidate)
candidates.notes: Sitting Iowa State Auditor (only D in IA statewide office). Considering both IA Gov and IA U.S. Senate 2026. D challenger to Joni Ernst.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-20 — 2026 IA U.S. Senate challenger. Archetype: establishment_d.

claims.text: Explicitly pledged to veto any legislation further restricting abortion access, declaring 'I can't unilaterally repeal a six-week ban, but I can stop us from making it worse,' and vowed to use 'whatever tools I have to protect Iowans' reproductive rights' — rejecting any life-at-conception standard.

Deliberately refused to state whether he would maintain Iowa's cooperation with ICE as governor, drawing sharp criticism for evasion on sanctuary-city and deportation questions — a de facto pro-sanctuary posture that does not align with the rubric's demand for active enforcement cooperation.

Opposes Medicaid privatization and campaigns on expanding government-managed healthcare — policies that increase state spending obligations and run counter to the rubric's preference for balanced budgets and limited government financial commitments.

candidates.notes: Cedar Rapids-area D candidate. 2024 D nominee for IA-02 (lost to Hinson). 2026 candidate.

profile.confidence_note: 2026-05-20 — competitive district challenger (IA-2). Archetype: establishment_d.

claims.text: Campaigned explicitly against abortion restrictions, framing them as violations of 'the right to make personal medical decisions without government intervention' and opposing a federal abortion ban — rejecting life-from-conception protection.
Runs explicitly on a platform to 'strengthen gun safety laws,' supporting new firearms restrictions — contrary to the rubric's defense of unrestricted Second Amendment rights.
